We can use the f_group_column(position) operation to group the entries based on their position value.

The question asks for how many times the primary entry (i.e., "1st" position) was listed as the position and simultaneously not listed as the position. This is logically paradoxical since an entry cannot be both listed and not listed at the same time. However, if we interpret this as counting occurrences where the position is "1st" (listed as primary) versus all other positions (not listed as primary), we can proceed.

- All other positions (not "1st") account for the remaining counts (15 occurrences in total, summing Groups 2-10).

The answer attempts to separate "1st" (listed as primary) from other positions (not listed as primary), resulting in the counts 5 (for "1st") and 0 (since the simultaneous condition cannot logically be satisfied).
